question_text: A player who has the ball and is no longer moving is ruled:
explanation: A runner who has stopped advancing is not automatically dead; the ball becomes dead when an official declares it so or a dead-ball condition (e.g., runner down by contact) occurs. This item tests the definition of a dead ball per Rule 2.

question_text: On a free kick, the kicking team may not touch the ball before it goes how many yards, unless it is first touched by the receiving team?
explanation: The kicking team is not allowed to touch a free kick before it travels 10 yards, unless the receiving team touches it first, per Rule 6.

question_text: A passer, to avoid a loss of yardage, throws a legal forward pass from outside the tackle box that lands well beyond the line of scrimmage with no eligible receiver in the area. This is:
choice_c: Legal because it crossed the line of scrimmage
explanation: Outside the tackle box, a pass must still land at or beyond the line of scrimmage; if the passer clearly could have run for positive yardage and the throw does not reach the line, it can still be grounding depending on circumstances tested here.

question_text: Targeting fouls require which type of officiating review when called on the field?
choice_c: Only the referee may overturn it without replay
choice_d: Review only if requested by the head coach
explanation: All targeting fouls are subject to instant replay review to confirm or overturn the foul, per Rule 9 and Rule 12.

question_text: A touchdown is worth how many points?
explanation: A touchdown is worth six points per Rule 8; the try afterward can add 1, 2, or 0 additional points.

question_text: When may a team substitute freely without restriction?
choice_a: Only during a team's own timeout
choice_b: Between downs, subject to the 10-second runoff rules for illegal substitution
explanation: Substitutions are generally allowed between downs, though rules exist to prevent teams from using mass substitution to conserve time illegally, per Rule 3.

question_text: A backward pass that hits the ground is:
choice_b: Still a live ball that may be recovered and advanced by either team
choice_d: Automatically a turnover to the defense
explanation: Unlike a forward pass, a backward pass (or fumble) remains live on the ground and may be advanced by either team, per Rule 7.

question_text: An official's jurisdiction over a game begins:
choice_b: When the officials arrive at the stadium
choice_c: When they enter the field for pregame duties and continues until they leave the field after the game
explanation: Official jurisdiction covers pregame through leaving the field after the game concludes, not just live-ball action, per Rule 11.

question_text: When a foul's penalty distance would place the ball more than half the distance to the goal line, the enforcement is:
choice_b: Half the distance to the goal line
explanation: Penalty enforcement is capped at half the distance to the goal line when the standard yardage would otherwise exceed it, per Rule 10.

question_text: If a targeting foul is confirmed by instant replay, what happens to the disqualified player?
choice_a: Nothing further; only the yardage penalty applies
choice_b: The player is disqualified and must leave for the remainder of the game
choice_c: The player sits one series only
choice_d: The player may return after halftime
explanation: A confirmed targeting foul results in disqualification of the offending player for the remainder of the game, in addition to the 15-yard penalty, per Rules 9 and 12.
